body.title: Librarian: fulltext search tool (files + palace + memories)
body.result: search() tool in search_tools.py — FTS palace+memories+tickets, ripgrep files, source filter, 21 tests — 4d85f878
body.description:
Add a low-inference search(query) tool to the Librarian that answers 'what do I know about X' across all indexed knowledge. Zero inference tokens in the hot path — search returns a compact ranked list; CC reads specific items only if it wants depth. Two backends already in place: (1) Postgres FTS — palace already has a GIN index; recall.py already uses plainto_tsquery; extend to cover clan.memories, adc.palace, and cc_queue tickets in one ranked query. (2) ripgrep for repo/file contents — subprocess rg call, top-N hits as file:line + 1-line snippet.

Result format is critical — compact ranked list, NOT full content. Target ~10-20 tokens per hit:
  decision: D-murderbot-feed-metaphor-2026-06-03 (0.94) — "Web UI naming follows Murderbot feeds..."
  file: devices/granny/daemon.py:57 (0.88) — "_ALERTED_IDS_FILE = _GRANNY_HOME..."
  palace: unseenuniversity/design/channel (0.81) — "shared channel = public broadcast..."
CC then calls Read/memory_get on specific items for depth. Initiation AND digestion cost both minimal.

Also support: search(query, source='files'|'palace'|'decisions') to narrow scope.

**Affected files:** devices/librarian/tools/ (new search_tool.py), devices/librarian/mcp_server.py (register tool), tests/librarian/
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; postgres FTS via plainto_tsquery; ripgrep via subprocess; result truncation enforced — no full-file dumps
**Scope boundary:** IN: unified search across palace+memories+tickets+files; compact ranked results; source filter. OUT: folder indexer/background sync (T-librarian-index-paths). OUT: git history (T-librarian-index-paths). OUT: semantic/vector search.
**Completion criteria:** mcp__librarian__search(query="murderbot") returns D-murderbot-feed-metaphor-2026-06-03 in top 3 within 200ms; result payload under 500 tokens for 10 hits; source filter narrows correctly; 6+ tests
